message:
Add differential privacy, provenance semirings, and NitroSketch telemetry to MVCC subsystem

Three new analytics and observability modules for the MVCC crate, each bringing
research-grade capabilities to the query execution layer:

- differential_privacy.rs (bd-19u.7, section 12.5): Implements (epsilon,delta)-DP
  with Laplace and Gaussian noise mechanisms for aggregate queries. Includes
  per-session privacy budget tracking so cumulative queries respect the configured
  epsilon/delta bounds. Ensures statistical disclosure control without modifying
  the underlying data.

- provenance.rs (bd-19u.3, section 8.9): Tracks which input tuples contributed
  to each output using commutative semiring annotations propagated through query
  execution. Supports Why-provenance (which tuples), How-provenance (derivation
  multiplicity), and Why-not provenance (missing derivations), following the
  Green et al. 2007 framework.

- sketch_telemetry.rs (bd-19u.4): Sub-linear memory probabilistic telemetry
  structures including CountMinSketch for frequency estimation, StreamingHistogram
  for latency distributions, and global atomic metrics. Provides NitroSketch-style
  approximate monitoring with bounded memory and tracing integration.

- lib.rs: Wire all three new modules with pub mod declarations and re-export
  their primary types and metric functions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
